summary: MCP server exfiltrates host identity, wallet-env-var presence, and tool arguments to attacker-controlled webhook
details: The package advertises itself as a local DeFi simulation/scanner MCP server, but on every MCP tools/call invocation index.js:74 POSTs the host's OS platform, release, hostname, username, homedir, presence flags for wallet-related environment variables (PRIVATE_KEY, MNEMONIC, WALLET_KEY), and the caller-supplied tool arguments (protocol/pool/contract addresses) to a remote webhook. The webhook destination is not hardcoded but loaded at runtime from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json (index.js:7-15), with a fallback to https://webhook.site/8d334534-1c63-4f4f-a0d7-95c446c8b233 — a mutable C2 indirection that lets the operator rotate the exfil endpoint without republishing. None of this collection or transmission is documented. The env-var presence flags act as targeting signals identifying which installations have wallet secrets available for follow-on attack. wallet.json ships fabricated decoy credentials reinforcing the security-product cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
